output.changeUnderReview: Section vertical padding updated from py-10 sm:py-16 (64px) to py-10 sm:py-24 (96px) to match Figma design

screenshots.figma: Figma node 785:33970 screenshot captured via MCP get_screenshot

figmaConfirmation.note: Figma design context (node 653:29365) explicitly shows py-[var(--margin/margin-7,96px)] on the Content wrapper. sm:py-24 = 96px is an exact match.

compositionAnalysis.overallShape: PASS - Dark charcoal section with rounded-3xl pseudo-element background. Updated vertical padding (sm:py-24 = 96px) now matches Figma's 96px vertical padding on the Content wrapper.
compositionAnalysis.majorElements: PASS - All major elements remain correctly positioned: ThemeHeading, controls row, card carousel. The increased padding does not displace or disrupt any element.
compositionAnalysis.relativeSizes: PASS - The increased top/bottom padding creates proportions that match the Figma reference. The breathing room above the eyebrow text and below the card row is visually consistent with the Figma design.
compositionAnalysis.visualWeight: PASS - The additional vertical padding gives the section appropriate visual weight and prevents the content from feeling cramped within the dark panel.
compositionAnalysis.overflow: PASS - No content overflow or clipping at any breakpoint after padding change.
compositionAnalysis.interactiveElements: PASS - Navigation arrows, progress bar, and buttons remain visible and unaffected by the padding change.

observations: Desktop (1440px): Section top/bottom padding is now 96px (sm:py-24), matching Figma's py-[var(--margin/margin-7, 96px)] exactly
Mobile (375px): Section padding remains at 40px (py-10), providing appropriate compact spacing for small screens
The padding change correctly uses the sm: breakpoint, so it applies from 640px and above
Internal spacing (heading margins, controls row gap, card gaps) remains unchanged and correct
No layout shifts or misalignment introduced by the padding change
All block variations (blog, team, resources, policies, featured posts, minimal, empty, long content) render correctly with the updated padding
The rounded dark background panel (before pseudo-element) extends correctly around the content with the new padding

variationChecks.notes: Padding increase applies evenly. Section looks proportionally correct.
Most closely matches Figma reference. Vertical padding now matches the 96px specified in the design.
Padding increase renders correctly with team member cards.
Padding increase renders correctly with resource cards.
Padding increase renders correctly with policy cards.
Padding increase renders correctly even without controls row.

notes.observation: The padding change from sm:py-16 (64px) to sm:py-24 (96px) is an exact match to the Figma design's --margin/margin-7 (96px). Visual comparison confirms the implementation now has the correct vertical breathing room matching the Figma reference.
